rulings.Case Title: BETTY PETERSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 26, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Cosco Fire Protection, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is taken off calendar. As to the exposure issue, Mr. Cantley¿s deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether Mr. Peterson was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (fireproofing) attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Cantley¿s declaration are overruled. Defendant¿s moving papers failed to properly make an issue of whether such exposures were a substantial factor in causing or contributing to Mr. Peterson¿s asbestos-related disease. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production on the substantial factor issue because its moving papers failed to demonstrate that it propounded contention discovery on plaintiffs asking for all evidence supporting their contention that any exposure attributable to defendant was a substantial factor, thus defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Even if defendant had sustained its burden, Dr. Bruch¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether any exposure attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to Mr. Peterson¿s asbestos-related disease. Defendant¿s moving papers failed to properly make an issue of whether it breached a duty owed to Mr. Peterson. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production because its moving papers failed to demonstrate that it propounded contention discovery on plaintiffs asking for all evidence supporting their contention that defendant owed a duty, and breached it, for affirmative actions of defendant¿s employees that allegedly exposed Mr. Peterson, as opposed to interrogatories regarding knowledge of asbestos hazards for exposure occurring on any premises owned, possessed and/or controlled by defendant, thus defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Even if defendant had sustained its burden, Dr. Cohen¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether defendant owed a duty to Mr. Peterson. Defendant¿s motion for summary adjudication is taken off calendar because plaintiffs represent that they have dismissed their strict liability and false representation causes of action and claim for punitive damages and those were the only issues defendant sought to have summarily adjudicated. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JERRY FULLMER VS. THOMAS ENGINEERING COMPANY
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 26, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Defendant Western Auto Supply Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of production under Aguilar. Defendant failed to negate an essential element of plaintiff¿s claim. Even if Mr. Wright¿s declaration is admissible, it does not demonstrate that the Western Auto store at issue was an independently owned dealer store that was not owned, operated or controlled by Western Auto. Further, even if paragraph 11 of Mr. Wright¿s declaration is admissible, it merely creates a triable issue when read in conjunction with plaintiff¿s deposition testimony. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
